page: Amazon Web Services x8i.96xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by Intel processors. It features 384 virtual CPUs mapped to 192 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. The dedicated CPUs operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. This instance is equipped with 6144.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core density of 16.0 GB per vCPU.

The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. It does not feature any local NVMe storage or integrated GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the massive memory footprint and high vCPU count, this server represents a high-density resource allocation. It is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, massive data analytics, and complex enterprise resource planning systems. The lack of local storage and GPU hardware makes it less suitable for local disk-heavy I/O operations or hardware-accelerated machine learning training.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.96xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by Intel processors running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 384 dedicated vCPUs across 192 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 6144.0 GB of system memory, yielding 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. It contains no local storage and zero GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, such as large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and massive data analytics pipelines that require substantial RAM capacity alongside high-frequency compute resources. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance for enterprise applications.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring three hundred eighty-four vCPUs and six terabytes of RAM for massive in-memory workloads.
